Metadata.title: ReadVC | Making every voice call visible
Metadata.description: Edge AI phone assistant for total accessibility and barrier-free calls.
Metadata.demoDescription: Discover all the features and capabilities of the ReadVC call assistant.
Metadata.problemDescription: Understand the invisible phone accessibility gap affecting millions.
Metadata.solutionDescription: Discover the technology behind ReadVC: Edge AI, Rust, and absolute privacy.

Footer.ctaSubtitle: Join the waitlist for early access.
Footer.tagline: Read Voice Call - Making Every Voice Call Visible.
Footer.builtWith: Made in Chile 🇨🇱. For the world 🌍.

hero.title: The first bidirectional solution for phone accessibility.
hero.subtitle: We restore autonomy to people with hearing loss or speech difficulties. Instant transcription and natural offline voice via Edge AI.
hero.stat1desc: People with hearing loss in Chile

problem.title: 100% of the phone network requires audio.
problem.subtitle: For over 700,000 people in Chile (and 1.5 billion worldwide), a simple emergency, medical, or work call is an insurmountable barrier.
problem.card1desc: Calls demand responses in under 2 seconds. Prolonged silence means they hang up on you.
problem.card2desc: Depending on a family member, friend, or human interpreter to schedule an appointment or do personal paperwork.
problem.card3desc: Current apps require fast internet, force you to use virtual numbers, or share your audio to the cloud.

solution.title: We automate sensory translation.
solution.subtitle: ReadVC replaces your phone's standard call screen, processing all audio locally without sending it to the internet.
solution.card1desc: We convert audio to text in under 200ms right on your screen. Understands local slang.
solution.card2desc: You type your response as if you were on WhatsApp. You control exactly what you say.
solution.card3desc: We inject your text converted into neural voice directly into the call, flowing naturally.
solution.card4desc: We use Edge AI (Rust/Kotlin models). Works without a data plan, safeguarding your absolute privacy.

howItWorks.title: This is what autonomy looks like.
howItWorks.subtitle: A familiar interface designed to reduce cognitive stress during the call.
howItWorks.step1desc: You see who is calling on screen and decide whether to answer. Total control over privacy.
howItWorks.step2desc: An automatic greeting warns: "Hello, I am hard of hearing. I'm using ReadVC, a text-to-speech assistant, I'll take a moment to answer..." (Adjustable auto message).
howItWorks.step3desc: What they say appears as text in real time. You write your reply with predictive typing help.
howItWorks.step4desc: Your text is instantly converted to natural voice. For the other side, the conversation flows normally.
howItWorks.mockupDoctorMsg1: Hi Hernán, how are you? I wanted to confirm your appointment tomorrow at...
howItWorks.mockupYouMsg: Yes doctor, I will be there at 3:30.

about.title: Built from real experience, not theory.
about.p1: ReadVC's founder and creator, Hernán Espinoza, is an IT Engineer and AWS Certified Developer with over 10 years of technical experience. He lives with profound bilateral hearing loss and knows firsthand what it means to struggle to make a call, the loss of job opportunities, and the frustrating reliance on third parties.
about.p2: ReadVC was born out of a personal need to solve the problem for thousands. It is not a market hypothesis; it is a 100% exclusive dedication to restoring autonomy through Artificial Intelligence (Edge AI), also serving as his university Capstone Project.
about.missionDesc: To break the barrier of silence in phone communications. We want 700 thousand people in Chile—and more than 430 million in the world—to be able to answer a call, express themselves, and feel that the world hears them, without relying on third parties and without compromising their privacy.
about.visionDesc: A world where hearing loss or speech difficulties are no barrier to communication. ReadVC is the bridge that transforms every phone into an accessible, decentralized terminal centered on the dignity of those who use it.

faq.title: Everything you need to know
faq.subtitle: We answer the most common questions about access, privacy, and how ReadVC works.
faq.q1: Why is this different from existing solutions?
faq.a1: Most platforms require an internet connection, a new phone number, or use cloud intermediaries. We process audio natively on your phone using AI, without requiring Wi-Fi or changing your mobile carrier.
faq.q2: What about the privacy of my calls?
faq.a2: Your privacy is guaranteed by design. All voice recognition (ASR) occurs locally (Offline) on your device via Edge AI. Your conversations never pass through our servers.
faq.q3: Will I have to pay to use the platform?
faq.a3: We will have a Freemium (free) version forever, designed to maximize social impact and help those who really need it. The most advanced corporate features will be paid.
faq.q4: Will it work with my current phone number?
faq.a4: Yes. ReadVC replaces the traditional calling screen of your Android system. You continue to receive and make calls using your current phone number and mobile plan.
faq.q5: When can I download the beta?
faq.a5: We are currently finalizing our closed Alpha testing phase. Join our waitlist to be the first to receive early access, stay updated on our progress, and get exclusive benefits.
faq.helpClearDoubts: We are here to help you clear any doubts.

contact.title: Get in touch.
contact.subtitle: Do you have questions, suggestions, or want to collaborate with us? Write to us and we will reply shortly.
contact.secure: Your information is safe with us.

Pricing.title: Sustainable Business Model.
Pricing.subtitle: A hybrid approach that guarantees massive free adoption for those who need it most, sustained by premium and corporate features.

freemium.title: Freemium
freemium.footer: Ideal for initial users and mass adoption (Word of mouth).

b2b.title: B2B Corporate
b2b.footer: Ideal for Clinics, Banks, and Call Centers.

hero.title: Everything ReadVC can do for you
hero.subtitle: Experience a real call and discover the full breakdown of our features.

interactive.title: Interactive Demo: How a call works
interactive.subtitle: Experience both sides of the conversation. In one panel you'll see the screen of the ReadVC user, and in the other, what the caller hears.
interactive.modalSubtitle: Start the call on the right phone to begin.
interactive.script1: Hello, I am hard of hearing. I'm using ReadVC, a text-to-speech assistant, I'll take a moment to answer... (Automated message)
interactive.script2: Hello! No problem. We are calling from the Medical Center to confirm your appointment tomorrow at 10:00 AM.
interactive.script3: Perfect. Remember to arrive 15 minutes early and bring your ID. Do you have any questions?
interactive.script4: Great, your appointment is confirmed. Have an excellent day. Goodbye.

replies.r1_1: Hello, thank you. I confirm my attendance.
replies.r1_2: Hello, I won't be able to attend.
replies.r2_1: Understood, I will be there early.

t1.title: Core Features
t1.subtitle: The Accessibility Bridge
t1.f1desc: The app 'listens' to the caller and transcribes instantly using local models (Whisper).
t1.f2desc: Type your reply and it converts to fluid natural voice in milliseconds (Piper) for the other side.
t1.f3desc: Intercepts and works over the traditional cellular network (GSM) as well as the internet (Wi-Fi).
t1.f4desc: Processing happens on the phone's chip. Works on the subway, in rural areas, or without a data plan.

t2.title: User Experience
t2.subtitle: The Daily Assistant
t2.f1desc: Replaces the traditional black screen with a visual WhatsApp-style interface. You see the call as messages.
t2.f2desc: Transcriptions are saved on the device so you don't forget medical instructions or directions.
t2.f3desc: Allows you to 'read' what an incoming call is about if you're in a meeting where you can't talk or listen.

t3.title: Premium Features
t3.subtitle: Scale Your Productivity
t3.f1desc: If the caller speaks English, you read it in Spanish; you answer in Spanish and they hear English.
t3.f2desc: Premium catalog of high-quality voices with different accents to represent your identity.
t3.f3desc: Encrypted synchronization of call history with Google Drive when changing phones.
t3.f4desc: Purchase 'on-demand' minute packages for translations without paying a monthly subscription.

t4.title: Security and Deep Tech
t4.subtitle: Unbreakable Architecture
t4.f1desc: The Rust core detects phone resources and 'swaps' AI models to avoid draining the battery.
t4.f2desc: No one but you can read your calls. Audio never travels to the cloud; everything happens on-device.
t4.f3desc: Local history and backups are encrypted with a key that only you possess.

cta.title: Sustainable Business Model
cta.subtitle: A hybrid approach that guarantees massive free adoption for those who need it most, sustained by premium and corporate features.

hero.title: 100% of the phone network requires audio.
hero.subtitle: It's not just a hearing or speech problem. It's a problem of synchrony, rights, and autonomy. Discover why current calls are an insurmountable wall.

groups.title: Who are we leaving out?
groups.subtitle: Over 700,000 people in Chile and 430+ million worldwide face this pain daily. Select a group to dive deeper.

g1.title: Deaf and Hard of Hearing Community
g1.subtitle: 100% of the cellular network requires audio.
g1.desc: The GSM network is natively sound-based. Conventional hearing aids often experience feedback, or digitized audio is incomprehensible. Current state aids (like VIAD) require human interpreters, violating privacy and limiting autonomy.
g1.i1: Inaccessibility to real emergencies (911 has no text fallback).
g1.i2: Loss of independence for personal medical appointments.

g2.title: Speech Disorders and Aphasias
g2.subtitle: The tyranny of 2 seconds.
g2.desc: They can hear perfectly, but their oral response may be slower, or they use esophageal voices (due to laryngectomies) that the phone distorts, making them incomprehensible to the caller.
g2.i1: If there's silence for more than 2 seconds, the caller assumes it dropped and hangs up.

g3.title: Neurodivergence and ASD
g3.subtitle: Sensory overload and telephobia.
g3.desc: Low-quality audio and the pressure to respond in real-time generate severe anxiety or sensory overload, leading to isolation. Current ASD laws do not cover technological tools for daily independence.
g3.i1: Lack of a 'cognitive buffer' to process information at their own pace.

gaps.title: The 3 Critical Gaps
gaps.gap1desc: Phone calls demand immediate responses. People with stuttering, aphasia, or who need a keyboard to reply require more processing time. By not responding quickly, the caller assumes a drop and ends the call.
gaps.gap2desc: Traditional cellular networks only transmit analog or encoded digital audio. They don't support text natively. This leaves those dependent on visual communication locked out of banking, health, and emergency systems that insist on voice validation.
gaps.gap3desc: State-provided solutions require human intermediaries (interpreters). An adult loses their autonomy if they have to ask their mother or a third party to help them call the doctor for a personal issue.

barriers.title: The World Still Runs on Voice
barriers.subtitle: The gap isn't just technological; it's about access to basic services. The inability to make a call excludes millions from:
barriers.b1desc: Recruiters prefer the agility of a phone call for the first filter. If you can't answer, they assume a lack of interest.
barriers.b2desc: Calling an ambulance or the police demands immediate oral communication. Time is life, and native text doesn't exist here.
barriers.b3desc: To unlock a card or confirm a suspicious transaction, the bank demands voice identity validation.
barriers.b4desc: Canceling internet service, coordinating package deliveries, or making complaints still require long phone waits.

competitors.title: Why do current solutions fail?
competitors.subtitle: Attempts exist, but none solve the root problem autonomously, quickly, universally, and 100% privately.
competitors.c1desc: Total loss of privacy. Requires scheduling, stable internet, and you depend on a third party (interpreter) for every personal matter.
competitors.c2desc: Only provides subtitles. Doesn't allow you to reply orally if you can't speak (it's not bidirectional). You only read how they talk to you.
competitors.c3desc: Restricted to expensive high-end phones. Its text mode isn't truly fluid for natural conversations.
competitors.c4desc: Limited and fragmented accessibility options. It doesn't allow fluid reading or injection directly into native GSM calls.

cta.title: Silence is not an option. See how we solve it.

hero.title: Enterprise-grade engineering, with a human purpose.
hero.subtitle: ReadVC is not just a transcriber. It's a complex Artificial Intelligence architecture operating entirely on your device (Edge AI), guaranteeing fluid, delay-free, and 100% private communications.

pillars.title: The Three Pillars of ReadVC
pillars.subtitle: We designed technology built for resilience. A failure during a critical call is not an option.
pillars.p1desc: In a phone conversation, every millisecond counts. We achieve latencies under 200ms in STT/TTS conversion thanks to optimized local AI models, allowing for a truly natural chat.
pillars.p2desc: Zero-Knowledge Architecture. Audio never travels to cloud servers. All processing happens in your phone's sandbox, protected with AES-256 encryption.
pillars.p3desc: Works on the native cellular network (GSM) or Wi-Fi. By not requiring a data plan to process audio (100% Offline), it guarantees functionality in rural or low-connectivity areas.

tech.title: 4-Layer Tech Stack
tech.subtitle: We've developed a hybrid engine combining the best Android user experience with the brutal performance of low-level AI languages.
tech.t1desc: Native call management in Android using InCallService without relying on third-party libraries or external clouds. The UI is built in Jetpack Compose for flawless 60fps performance.
tech.t2desc: Zero-copy transfer between the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) and native code. Raw audio buffers travel directly to the AI without intermediate memory copies, saving valuable milliseconds.
tech.t3desc: The core of all audio logic is written in Rust. This ensures flawless memory management, totally free of the Garbage Collector, guaranteeing audio without micro-cuts or unpredictable latencies.
tech.t4desc: Highly optimized local generative models quantized to Int8. Capable of performing STT and TTS inference using less than 300MB of the mobile phone's RAM.

bento.title: Why use Rust in the Mobile Backend?
bento.subtitle: Processing audio and running live AI inside a cell phone requires low-level engineering. Rust is the only reasonable choice.
bento.b1desc: Rust guarantees memory safety at compile time. There is no Garbage Collector (GC) pausing execution, which is crucial to avoid frame or audio drops during long calls.
bento.b2desc: Metal-level performance for real-time AI inference, allowing us to achieve <200ms latencies on the device itself.
bento.b3desc: 70% of the core code (the AI engine and audio logic) will be directly reused in our future iOS version (Swift) thanks to Rust's cross-compilation capabilities.
bento.b4desc: Modern, secure, and mature support for local machine learning thanks to powerful crates like Burn, Candle, and ndarray.
